items.title: WorldCache: Content-Aware Caching for Accelerated Video World Models
items.authors: Umair Nawaz, Ahmed Heakl, Ufaq Khan et al.
items.abstract: Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) power high-fidelity video world models but remain computationally expensive due to sequential denoising and costly spatio-temporal attention. Training-free feature caching accelerates inference by reusing intermediate activations across denoising steps; however, existin...

items.title: End-to-End Training for Unified Tokenization and Latent Denoising
items.authors: Shivam Duggal, Xingjian Bai, Zongze Wu et al.
items.abstract: Latent diffusion models (LDMs) enable high-fidelity synthesis by operating in learned latent spaces. However, training state-of-the-art LDMs requires complex staging: a tokenizer must be trained first, before the diffusion model can be trained in the frozen latent space. We propose UNITE - an autoen...

items.title: ThinkJEPA: Empowering Latent World Models with Large Vision-Language Reasoning Model
items.authors: Haichao Zhang, Yijiang Li, Shwai He et al.
items.abstract: Recent progress in latent world models (e.g., V-JEPA2) has shown promising capability in forecasting future world states from video observations. Nevertheless, dense prediction from a short observation window limits temporal context and can bias predictors toward local, low-level extrapolation, maki...

items.title: VideoDetective: Clue Hunting via both Extrinsic Query and Intrinsic Relevance for Long Video Understanding
items.authors: Ruoliu Yang, Chu Wu, Caifeng Shan et al.
items.abstract: Long video understanding remains challenging for multimodal large language models (MLLMs) due to limited context windows, which necessitate identifying sparse query-relevant video segments. However, existing methods predominantly localize clues based solely on the query, overlooking the video's intr...
